patterns_recognized: BAL@HOU is now a third straight appearance with HOU favored: 7/17 BAL won 3-2 as the sole staked GOLD CONFIRM, 7/18 BAL won 4-2 again (correctly SKIPped). Today's stack is the cleanest yet (score=5, all four dimensions affirm, no caps fire) but two real consecutive losses for a favored HOU against different BAL starters each time is a team-level pattern the signal stack can't see. DOWNGRADE applied.

HARD CAP 7 (pick-side bullpen disadvantage) has now false-negatived on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap values of +0.018 (KC@BAL 7/10), +0.0189 (7/12), +0.0185 (MIA@MIL 7/18) -- all BAL/MIL wins. Today's TB@BOS fires the same cap at an even smaller +0.0117 on a pre-cap A- stack (SP+PQI+Stage all agree). Per the 7/18 postgame's own 'actionable, not just monitoring' flag, this is treated as a same-side tier restoration (OVERRIDE, not a pick flip) rather than a fifth silent confirmation of a cap under active review.

MIA@MIL's F3_swing_take_gap (3225.7), F5_bullpen_gap (+0.86), and hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap (0.0185) are numerically identical to the values the 7/18 postgame reported for this same matchup, despite different game_pk and starters (Gasser/Perez) listed today. This looks like a stale/duplicated feature computation rather than fresh data -- flagging for pipeline verification; treated the pick with extra caution regardless.

Seven picks today (TEX@ATL, PIT@CLE, SD@KC, MIN@CHC, DET@LAA, SF@SEA, STL@ARI) are D-grade with SP-xERA and PQI both opposing the pick -- the archive's most reliable SKIP-confirmation shape since 7/9. Consistent with 7/9-7/18 findings, none of these were faded to the opposing side; SKIP was confirmed as-is.

CIN@COL produced two consecutive 7+ run blowouts in opposite directions (7/17, 7/18) against a dual-opposing SKIP signal, flagged as a possible Coors park-factor blind spot. Today's CIN@COL is structurally different -- a clean, confirming B+ stack with no dual opposition -- so it was CONFIRMed at GOLD rather than downgraded on the strength of an unrelated recent-variance flag.

WSH@OAK swung from a 23-4 WSH win (7/17) to a 15-1 OAK win (7/18); the 7/18 postgame explicitly warned not to treat either result as a prior. Today's SKIP was confirmed on its own thin, slightly-negative-edge merits without reference to either recent blowout.

BAL @ HOU.reasoning: Mechanically the cleanest stack on the slate (score=5: F-signal, SP 0.47 xERA agrees, PQI +6.0 confirms, Stage agree; no caps fire). But this is BAL's third straight appearance as underdog against a favored HOU: 7/17 BAL won 3-2 as the sole staked GOLD CONFIRM, 7/18 BAL won 4-2 again. Two consecutive real losses with different HOU/BAL starters each time suggests a team-level pattern the signal stack doesn't capture. pick_prob=0.644 also sits in the >65% home-favorite-caution zone. DOWNGRADE to SKIP.
